headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	The minister was accused of making utterances that could **increase** rather than ................. anxiety among the citizen
2.	At the end of the crisis, the leader declared that there was no **victor** and no .................
3.	I was impressed by the man’s **eagerness** but was rather put off by his wife’s ...........
4.	He is a **famous** artist but has a ............... drinking habit
5.	The first twin is so **vain** and the second so .................... that one would think they were not brothers
7.	To **ascend** this steep hill is the problem, to ................ is quite easy
8.	Working in the field is easy when your machete is **sharp** and not when it is .............
9.	The **gradual** process of transition to civil rule came to an .................... end
10.	The **exterior** and the ................... parts of the fruit remain very hard even after it is ripe

S2.headd: From the words labelled A to E, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 11.	The committee set up to investigate the cause of the riot has called for .................. from the public
12.	The Satellite maintains a constant ....................... round the earth
13.	The chairman called him to ................. when his comments were getting off the point
14.	His ............... came through only a few days before he was to be executed
15.	The power failure .............. normal services for days
16.	The interest of the students should be of ............... importance to any school administrator
17.	My father wanted to buy a car but the prices of those available were not within his ...................
18.	The robbers .................. all the rooms, looking for jewels
19.	We reasoned that the kidnappers might kill the young girl if we didn’t pay the ....................
20.	The players would expect some form of ................. after working hard to win the cup
22.	The old man took his sons to the zoo as a special New Year ...................

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations of the sentences is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider the most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 23.	Although Etiebet had earlier insisted that he was right, he later ate his words. This means that he later

opts.q1: B. admitted that he was wrong
opts.q2: C. apologized for being so rude
opts.q3: D. realized that he was more correct than he taught

S3.qs: 24.	The young girl swept the board at the recent competitions. This means that the young girl

opts.q0: A. came last in the competitions
opts.q1: B. cleaned the score-board very well
opts.q2: C. won some of the competitions
opts.q3: D. won everything that could be won

S3.qs: 25.	The listeners were swept off their feet by the politician's arguments. This means that the politician

opts.q0: A. failed to impress the people
opts.q3: D. told them things they didn’t expect

S3.qs: 26.	I was struck by the broad sweep of her argument. This means that her argument

opts.q0: A. covered all parts of	the subject
opts.q1: B. focused only on a specific aspect of the subject
opts.q2: C. was well presented and in good language
opts.q3: D. was poorly presented and not well focused

S3.qs: 27.	The party was really going with a swing when somebody called me out. This means that when I was called out the party was
28.	She started the game poorly but later turned the tables on her opponent. This means that, she

opts.q0: A. remained a poor player and lost
opts.q1: B. changed her position of play and so won

S3.qs: 29.	The boys ran away with their tails between their legs. This shows that the boys left
30.	He said he was a master of electronics, but I knew he was talking through his hat. This means that I knew he was

opts.q0: A. relying on someone else’s ideas
opts.q1: B. merely relying on what he had studied
opts.q3: D. claiming what he knew nothing about

S3.qs: 31.	My brother’s driving could make one tear one's hair out. This means that his driving could make one
32.	It is really hard to come to terms with going blind, This means that if one goes blind one

opts.q0: A. finds it easy to accept
opts.q1: B. has no choice but to adjust
opts.q2: C. finds it difficult to accept
opts.q3: D. gradually gets used to it

S4.headd: From the words or groups of words labeled AtoE below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words.

S4.qs: 34.	Flight WT509 from Lagos **terminates** in Maiduguri
35.	Her **terse** remarks brought the conversation to an end
36.	The orison officials usually **torture** the inmates
37.	I would regard what he has done as a **treacherous** act
38.	I don’t agree that the problem is **intractable**
39.	This problem may prove rather **tricky** for the government
40.	The Iona war ended when **an armistice** was agreed upon
41.	The assassination of the statesman threw the country into a state of **turmoil**
42.	To become an artisan one has to undergo a period of **apprenticeship**

S5.headd: From the words or group of words labeled A to D, choose the one that best completes each of the following sentences

S5.qs: 43.	This is an improvement ................... your last essay
44.	The team has suffered defeat ................. defeat
45.	Unemployment has been ................ the increase since last year
46.	Cats sleep ................. day and hunt at night
47.	I don’t want anything to interfere .............. my project
48.	The two brothers came in ............... the back door
49.	...................... the terms of the agreement with your landlord, you should not sub-let the house
50.	Nigeria's economy was buoyant ................ the 1970s
51.	The musician ................... waxed that record died fifty years ago
52.	The author ............... book you are reading now, won the Nobel Prize for Literature last year
53.	The lady .............. you were talking about yesterday is here now
54.	The pencil .............. you gave me yesterday is lost
55.	I honestly don’t understand ............... you hate that man
56.	I’d like to know .................. he'll wriggle out of this case
57.	................. of the two pencils is yours?
58	.............. university is ............... institution of higher learning
59.	I’d give up smoking, if I ................ you
60.	It is high time you ................ visiting that friend of yours
61.	.................. had he entered when it started to rain
62.	All your ................. like you very much
63.	I’m sure this bucket is ................
64.	That uncle of .............. ought to see you through this
65.	............... of what he said is incorrect
66.	He comes here ................. often
67.	He is always asking for more .................?
68.	It was he who said he didn't fear death ...............?
69.	He said he didn’t fear death ...................?
70.	The government has a special programme for .................

S6.headd: Instruction: In the following passages the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage five choices are offered in columns labelled A to E. For each question, choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
